title: Qorche Task Definition
description: Defines a project with a DAG of tasks for concurrent filesystem orchestration. Qorche coordinates parallel workers with MVCC-inspired conflict detection.

project.description: Project name. Used in snapshots, WAL entries, and output.

runners.description: Named runner configurations. Each key is a runner name that tasks can reference via the 'runner' field. When omitted, all tasks use the default CLI runner.

tasks.description: Ordered list of task definitions forming a DAG. Tasks execute in dependency order, with independent tasks running in parallel.

type.description: Runner type identifier. Determines which AgentRunner implementation is used.

endpoint.description: Service endpoint URL (e.g. for Ollama or remote runners).

extra_args.description: Additional command-line arguments passed to the runner process.

allowed_commands.description: Permitted executables for shell runners. Required when type is 'shell'.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum execution time in seconds per task invocation.

task.description: A single task in the execution DAG.

id.description: Unique identifier within the project. Used as the DAG node key and in log filenames.

instruction.description: Free-text instruction passed to the runner. For shell runners, this is the command to execute.
examples: Refactor the auth module to use JWT tokens

type.description: Classification of work. Used for reporting and future scheduling heuristics.

depends_on.description: IDs of tasks that must complete successfully before this task starts. Forms the DAG edges.

files.description: Declared file scope - paths this task is expected to modify. Used for scoped snapshots and scope-violation auditing. Supports files and directories.

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts when this task loses an MVCC conflict. 0 means no retries.

runner.description: Name of the runner to use for this task, referencing a key in the top-level 'runners' map. When omitted, the default CLI runner is used.
